criteria.label: State Bayes' theorem and derive it from the definition of conditional probability.
criteria.example: Write P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) and P(B|A) = P(A∩B)/P(A), set P(A∩B) = P(B|A)P(A), substitute to get P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).

criteria.label: Compute a posterior using the law of total probability for P(B).
criteria.example: Given P(S)=0.01, P(F|S)=0.90, P(F|ham)=0.05: compute P(F)≈0.0585 and P(S|F)≈0.154; show both arithmetic steps.

criteria.label: Distinguish P(A|B) from P(B|A) and explain the prosecutor's fallacy.
criteria.example: Explain that P(+|disease)=0.99 does not imply P(disease|+)≈0.99 when prevalence is 0.1%; compute the true posterior ≈1.9%.

criteria.label: Apply Bayes' theorem to a two-hypothesis numeric problem and verify posteriors sum to 1.
criteria.example: With priors [0.5, 0.5] and likelihoods [0.117, 0.201], compute evidence=0.159, posteriors ≈ [0.368, 0.632], and confirm they sum to 1.
